{{Quote|His crippling of one of our craft in Ichalin Station some months ago did not turn out so positively: four of our valued agents in that area were captured and killed when their escape craft proved inoperable.|Addendum by Lieutenant Commander Bakki Sourthol to Rebel Alliance SecuriDex datafile on Yansan|<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref>}}
'''Ichalin Station''' was a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] situated in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Hijoian Space/Legends|Hijoian Space]]. In an example described in the ''[[Bounty Hunters Guild Handbook]]'', a [[Unidentified Glottalphib|Glottalphib]] [[pyromania]]c [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggled]] a [[CZ-28 Flamestrike flame cannon|flamethrower]] through Ichalin Station customs, earning himself a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] posting. Around [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], the [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]] [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] [[Yansan]] [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotaged]] a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] present in Ichalin Station, leading to the capture and [[Death/Legends|death]] of the Rebel agents attempting to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] the crippled vessel.

==Description==
Ichalin Station was a [[Space station/Legends|space station]]<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|76|Ichalin Station}}</ref> located in a [[Ichalin Station system/Legends|star system]] that constituted a part of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Hijoian Space/Legends|Hijoian Space]].<ref name="EAOC Ichalin" />

==History==
{{Quote|He gets away, but earns another price on his head when he sneaks a CZ-28 Flamestrike through Ichalin Station customs.|Arack Dostryt, on a hypothetical Glottalphib in an example from the ''Bounty Hunters Guild Handbook''|<ref name="BHC">''[[The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Yansan.png|thumb|right|200px|The terrorist Yansan sabotaged a Rebel Alliance starship in Ichalin Station.]]
When [[Author/Legends|composing]] a section for an edition of the ''[[Bounty Hunters Guild Handbook]]'' published<ref name="BHC" /> at some point between [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="19 BBY 0 BBY">''[[The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett]]'' includes an [[in-universe]] copy of the ''[[Bounty Hunters Guild Handbook]]''. The included edition of the handbook was published at some point following the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and is annotated by [[Greedo/Legends|Greedo]], whose [[Death/Legends|death]] is depicted in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. Since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the formation of the Empire and the events of ''A New Hope'' in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], respectively, the included edition of the ''Bounty Hunters' Guild Handbook'' must have been published at some point between those two dates.</ref> [[Arack Dostryt]], the [[Director of Recruiting]] for the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]], described a hypothetical example meant to illustrate the fact that [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] could collect multiple posted [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] for the same captured individual. In the example story, a [[Unidentified Glottalphib|Glottalphib]] [[pyromania]]c [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggled]] a [[CZ-28 Flamestrike flame cannon]] through Ichalin Station customs, leading to a bounty being placed on him. The Glottalphib of the example subsequently decided to maintain a low profile but nevertheless later earned an additional bounty posting on himself due to an unrelated reason.<ref name="BHC" />

At some point by [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="2 ABY">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] used by four agents of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] was present in Ichalin Station. During that [[Time/Legends|time]], the [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]] [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] [[Yansan]] [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotaged]] the ship, leading to the capture and [[Death/Legends|death]] of the agents when they were unable to operate the crippled vessel in an escape attempt. Rebel [[Lieutenant Commander/Legends|Lieutenant Commander]] [[Bakki Sourthol]] subsequently mentioned the Ichalin Station incident in an addendum to an Alliance [[SecuriDex]] [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] on Yansan.<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ichalin Station was first mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="AIR" /> The [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' placed both Ichalin Station<ref name="CSWE" /> and the [[Hijoian Docks]]—another [[Realspace/Legends|space]] station<ref name="CSWE Hijoian">{{CSWECite|II|42|Hijoian Docks}}</ref> connected to Yansan and also introduced in ''Alliance Intelligence Reports''<ref name="AIR" />—in the [[Fakir sector/Legends|Fakir sector]].<ref name="CSWE" />

The [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', however, placed Ichalin Station's [[Star system/Legends|star system]] and the [[Hijoian system]] in grid square J-8 as part of the Expansion Region's Hijoian Space while the Fakir [[Sector/Legends|sector]] was placed in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> This article assumes the latter placement is correct for Ichalin Station.
